history.content:
<thought>
In my current view, the cell directly in front of me is a wall (#). To move toward any open space, I should turn so I can avoid the wall; turning right will likely face an open corridor rather than the wall.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see lava (L) directly ahead in my current facing direction, so moving forward would be deadly. The cells to the left and right aren’t visible clearly, but to avoid lava I should first change orientation. I will turn right to look for a safe path toward the goal/unknown area.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see only unknown cells in front of me; the floor/goal area appears to be to my left where there is open space next to a wall. Since the cell directly in front is unknown and I need to find safe paths, I’ll rotate left to look for navigable cells rather than move into uncertainty.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
In my current view, lava (L) is directly in front of me (the cell immediately above ^ is L), so I should not move forward. To find a safe path, I will rotate left to change direction and look for a non-lava floor or the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I can see a wall column (#) to the left of me; moving forward would go into a wall. I should turn right to face the open area (dots) and avoid the visible lava cells.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
In the current view, the cell directly in front of me (above `^`) is `L` (lava), so I must not move forward. To find a safe route toward the goal, I should rotate to face a different neighboring cell rather than moving into lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can’t see the goal or any lava yet; most of the view is unknown. Ahead of me appears to be unknown, so I should start exploring by moving forward into the visible open space.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I can’t see the goal or any safe path yet—most of the map is unknown. To explore systematically and avoid unseen hazards, I’ll start by turning left to change my facing direction and look for openings near the wall.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I see the agent `^` near the bottom of the view. The cell directly ahead is `#` (a wall), so moving forward is impossible. To find a safe route, I should change my facing direction. I will turn right to look for a non-wall, non-lava cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The only visible structure is directly in front: the cell ahead is a wall (#), so moving forward is impossible. I should turn to find an open (.) or the goal (G) once it comes into view.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can’t see the goal yet (everything is unknown `?`), but the cell directly in front of me appears to be `L` (lava), which is deadly. To avoid it, I’ll turn right to look for a safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I see the goal is not in my current view. The cell directly in front of me appears to be a wall (#), so moving forward is not possible. I should turn right to look for an open (.) path away from the wall.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see lava (L) directly in multiple forward-facing cells, and the immediate cell in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. To find a safe path, I’ll rotate right to scan for a floor or the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can only see the immediate neighborhood. Ahead of me is currently unknown (`?`), and I also see walls (`#`) to my left in this view. To progress toward the map and possibly find the goal, I’ll start by turning right to check what’s accessible.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can only see unknown cells except for the bottom row. My forward cell (just above the bottom boundary) is unknown, but moving forward is the only way to make progress toward the goal; there is no visible wall in front and the only visible lava is not directly in front. I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I can’t see the goal yet (it’s all unknown above). In front of me is lava at the visible edge, so moving forward would be fatal. With no safe forward options visible, I’ll rotate to look for a non-lava direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see walls immediately to my left and the column of lava ahead-left/right around the center area, but the cell directly in front of me (one step forward) is floor '.' rather than lava or a wall. The safest immediate move is to go forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
